title: pimelea flava subsp. flava
intro: Pimelea flava subsp. flava, also known as yellow rice flower, is a flowering plant species native to Australia, characterized by its bright yellow, star-shaped flowers and small, oval-shaped leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in Australian bush medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to treat colds, fever, and respiratory issues due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Pimelea flava subsp. flava include its potential to alleviate symptoms of bronchitis, coughs, and sore throats, as well as its ability to boost the immune system. Pimelea flava subsp. flava can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
